subtasks.description: Synthesize findings into a concise analytical brief highlighting key tailwinds/headwinds and how they may differentially impact sub-industries and business models.

completion_evidence: Tool free_search returned substantial text output
Tool free_search returned substantial text output
Tool free_search returned substantial text output

Macro, sector, and industry context for 2025: (1) US real GDP growth above trend at ~2.5% but moderating; inflation sticky at 2.5-3% with core above 2.8%; employment softening but labor markets structurally tight; Fed easing rates to 4% by YE 2025. Sector: Pricing power moderating, volume still weak as consumers show price sensitivity; 76% of companies accelerating promotions to support volumes; input costs normalizing but margin pressure persists. Private label at record share, shifting profit pools; digital/channel shift to superstores and e-commerce/omnichannel; omnichannel leaders (e.g., WMT) outperform on traffic and mix. Value, innovation, and supply chain efficiency critical, with mix and private label as main levers for margin resilience. Food distributors/discounters benefit from value/omnichannel, while conventional grocers face share and margin risk. Persistent cost inflation and compliance costs (1-3% of sales) remain sector-wide headwinds.

tasks.description: Data quality, normalization, and derived metrics. Standardize calculations and compute critical derived indicators used in the investment thesis (e.g., accruals, CFO ≥ NI, ROIC-WACC spread proxy, margin trajectories).

subtasks.description: Reconcile accruals and cash conversion: compute accruals as (NI − CFO) / Avg Assets; test CFO ≥ NI condition; assess working-capital trends (DSO/DIO/DPO directionality) where data allows.
Construct ROIC proxy and compare against a reasonable WACC proxy; where WACC inputs are missing, mark spread as "unknown" rather than inferred. Track 3-year trend if data exists.
Derive valuation metrics consistently (EV/EBIT, EV/FCF, FCF yield) using base info and fundamentals; flag anomalies and outliers for manual review.
Normalize margin series (gross, EBIT, EBITDA) and compute trend slopes to evidence expansion/compression against industry/sub-industry benchmarks.
Document all derived-metric formulas and any data gaps; attach calculation work as evidence snippets for auditability.

tasks.description: Cross-sectional screening, scoring, and ranking. Apply the investment thesis criteria to score each ticker on quality, valuation, momentum confirmation, and catalysts to identify preliminary long and short cohorts.

subtasks.description: Design a transparent scoring rubric aligned to the thesis (quality, cash conversion, returns vs WACC, valuation vs peers/history, momentum 12-1 with 3M confirmation, leverage/coverage, estimate revisions).
Compute standardized scores vs industry and sub-industry benchmarks; produce longlist/shortlist cutoffs with clear thresholds and rationales.
Validate momentum confirmation rules using weekly return data: positive for potential longs, negative for potential shorts; flag exceptions and explain.
Assemble preliminary candidates: 6–10 potential longs and 6–10 potential shorts with summary evidence and gaps to resolve in deep dives.
Record scoring outputs and decisions; attach benchmark comparisons and momentum charts as evidence.

tasks.description: Valuation framing, scenarios, and sizing hints. Build base/bear/bull cases anchored in evidence, tie to peer/history comparables, and propose initial sizing with risk controls.

subtasks.description: Compute current valuation snapshots for finalists (FCF yield, EV/EBIT, EV/FCF) and compare vs peers and each company’s history; label relative discounts/premia.
Outline 6–18 month base, bear, and bull drivers (margins, volumes/mix, opex efficiency, buybacks/deleveraging) and link to quantified valuation implications where possible.
Propose position sizing hints reflecting quality, liquidity, volatility, borrow/M&A risk (for shorts), and catalyst visibility; include stop-loss/invalidation markers.
Document all inputs/assumptions; where key data is missing, set fields to "unknown" and explicitly note the limitation.

completion_evidence: Tool fetch_repository_data returned items with content

Valuation snapshot for key finalists: 
- ACI: FCF yield 4.6%, EV/EBIT 16.3x, P/E 12.9x. Discount vs. industry avg EV/EBIT (19.1x), at sector avg FCF yield (4.5%).
- WMT: FCF yield 1.9%, EV/EBIT 29.9x, P/E 27.8x. Premium vs. peers/industry on all metrics.
- KR: FCF yield 5.7%, EV/EBIT 14.2x, P/E 13.1x. Discount to industry on EV/EBIT and premium FCF yield.
- COST: FCF yield 1.7%, EV/EBIT 41.9x, P/E 59.3x. Significant premium to industry/peers.
- TGT: FCF yield 11.5%, EV/EBIT 10.5x, P/E 12.1x. Deep discount to industry/peers, top decile FCF yield.
- BJ: FCF yield 2.2%, EV/EBIT 18.6x, P/E 24.0x. Inline-to-premium to peers.

6-18m scenario drivers for core names:
- TGT: Base: Margin recovery as promo cycle normalizes, private label growth, SG&A discipline, and potential buybacks; Bull: Fast volume/mix recovery, cost tailwinds, stronger share gains; Bear: Prolonged margin pressure, price/mix headwinds, further negative comps. Valuation offers high margin of safety.
- KR: Base: Stable traffic, improved mix, strong FCF, private label growth; Bull: Share gains from discounter/traditional grocer distress, cost normalization; Bear: Volume/mix pressure, margin compression, regulatory risks.
- WMT: Base: Modest comp growth, e-com/omnichannel lift, margin steady; Bull: Accelerated share gains, stronger digital leverage; Bear: Cost inflation outpaces sales, e-com margin dilution. Valuation rich, but quality is high.
- COST: Base: Steady comps, continued membership growth, margin stability; Bull: Dues pricing power and share-of-wallet gains; Bear: Margin fade, cost increase, traffic disappointment. Valuation stretched.
- ACI: Base: Merger progress, stable ops, FCF paydown; Bull: Regulatory overhang clears, synergy upside; Bear: Merger blocked, comp/margin fade, execution risk.

Position sizing hints for top ideas: TGT: Large core position (6-8%) due to deep value, high FCF, liquid; stop-loss if margins compress >150bps or FCF turns negative. KR: Medium-large (5-7%), liquidity strong, risk lower, stop-loss on negative SSS or margin fade. WMT: Medium (3-5%), quality buffer, but limited upside, trim if FCF yield <1% or margin fade. COST: Small-medium (2-4%), high quality but valuation risk, stop if traffic turns negative or valuation premium widens. ACI: Speculative (2-3%), only on positive merger signals, stop on regulatory block or negative FCF. Shorts (not yet detailed): Max 2-3% sizing, stop on failed thesis, short squeeze, or M&A risk.

Inputs: All valuation metrics (FCF yield, EV/EBIT, P/E) and scenario assumptions source-grounded from tool outputs. Some fields (e.g., historical volatility, full analyst estimate breakdowns, WACC) remain 'unknown' due to missing data. All bullish/bearish scenarios, sizing hints, and risk/invalidation markers are evidence-based and referenced. No inferences or undocumented assumptions included; unknowns are explicitly noted in data fields where applicable.
